ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-14-2016 Continued To Mar-21-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Myers for Diana Starnes. Jerome Grimes (via court call) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it thought the accounting was to be submitted without further hearing. Mr. Grimes talked about the US Bank account, the Chase Bank account, the civil matter, that he has done his own investigation, that he has an issue with the attorney's regarding the petition, that he receives information late, that there are tax issues, that there is no guarantee of funds, that Diana Starnes does not know the law, that he was not issued letters, that if there is a law suit he will need to contact an attorney, talked about escheated money, that in regards to taxes they could save 50% by calling the IRS and explaining the issue, that the administrator stole his car, that they need an independent investigation, that there is an issue with the tax preparer and that he has been intimidated. The Court stated that Mr. Grimes appeared telephonically with a variety of oral objections, that the matter will be under submission as of March 21, 2016 based on the written/filed objections and oral objections from today. Mr. Myers stated that the omnibus clause and order should cover the money, that regarding the tax issue brought up by Mr. Grimes it could be done if resources were not available but they are. The Court stated that the argument is over, that the parties are to file any objections and that it needs a revised order.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos & Deputy Neves. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Feb-29-2016 Continued To Mar-14-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Myers for (personal representative) Diana Starnes.

The court asked if there were any way to get an estimate on taxes. Mr. Myers stated that they have 2010, that 2012 had a liability, that in regards to the 2014 taxes the fiduciary does not want to prepare them until payment is made but they are unable to pay him/her until the taxes are done, that Mr. Myers himself does not do tax returns at this time, that they will need to find someone to do the taxes and the resources to pay and that a ballpark figure was included in his declaration of $7,000.00. The Court asked if there was additional information to submit. Mr. Myers indicated that he would have additional information on the extraordinary fees and the potential surcharge once they get the discretionary figures. The Court requested that a proposed order be submitted by March 7, 2016, that the accounting petition and the bifurcated issues will be continued to March 14, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and that if everything is in order the matter will be under submission as of that date.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-03-2016 Continued To Feb-29-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Mraule & Peter Myers for and with (administrator) Diana Starnes.

The Court asked Ms. Starnes to clarify her phone calls prior to the last hearing date. Ms. Starnes reported that she disapproved of the extraordinary fees, that it does not leave her family with anything, that they were forced to sell the home as Mr. Mraule did not comply with paperwork requests timely, that they could have kept the house and that the attorney was supposed to look out for her and her family. The Court asked how to steer this, that if the petition is not verified by Ms. Starnes is it properly before it and how to proceed. Mr. Mraule stated that Ms. Starnes verified the initial petition with the same requests and that they spent a great amount of time on the appeals that her brother filed. Mr. Myers stated that in regards to the house issue Ms. Starnes attempted to carry the mortgage, that Wells Fargo was going to foreclose, that they attempted to sell it prior to the foreclosure, that there were liens against the decedent, that dealing with her brother was tough, that the firm stopped tracking their time 3 months ago, that the request for $48,500.00 is low, that efforts were made to keep the house and that everyone did what they could. The court asked if Ms. Starnes does not verify the petition what is the right approach. Mr. Myers cited Bresler v. Wong, stating that it was marginally similar. The Court asked Ms. Starnes what she wants today and if she is still objecting. Ms. Starnes stated that she tried to obtain new counsel, that "Keep Your Home California" was going to let them keep the house but the documents were not submitted, that the family is now displaced and that the attorneys looked out for themselves but not her family. The Court told counsel that they could file their own petition without verification. Mr. Myers stated that Ms. Starnes has articulated her objections here today, that she wants the court to make the decision and that her issues are: that the firm did not sufficiently represent her family in regards to the residence, that too much time was spent defending the appeals and that too much time was spent on the vexatious litigant piece. Ms. Starnes stated that she agrees to submit the case and that she spoke to the Bar Association who told her that it was up to the Judge. The Court stated that it could give Ms. Starnes a chance to file written objections. Mr. Myers suggested that they create a verification page and submit it. Ms. Starnes stated that she will sign the verification if she understands what it is.
